title: Why Employees Resent Team-Building Exercises
subtitle: 员工为何抵触团建活动

paragraphs.en: Team-building exercises have become popular for managers trying to increase organisational and team harmony and productivity. Unfortunately many employees resent compulsory bonding and often regard these exercises as a nuisance. A paper published this week by University of Sydney researchers in Social Networks has reported participants' feelings about team-building interventions, revealing ethical implications in forcing employees to take part. "Many people see team building activities as a waste of time, so we decided to look in more depth at what's behind this," said the paper's lead researcher, Dr Peter Matous.
paragraphs.zh: 对于试图提升组织和团队凝聚力及生产力的管理者来说，团队建设活动已变得颇为流行。但不幸的是，许多员工对强制性的团建联谊心怀不满，并常常把这些活动视为一件麻烦事。悉尼大学的研究人员本周在《社交网络》期刊上发表的一篇论文，报告了参与者对团建干预措施的感受，揭示了强迫员工参与其中的道德问题。该论文的首席研究员彼得·马图斯博士说："很多人认为团建活动是浪费时间，所以我们决定深入探究这背后的原因。"

paragraphs.en: "Teams are formed, combined and restructured. Staff are relocated and office spaces redesigned. All this is done with the aim of improving workplace efficiency, collaboration and cohesion. But does any of this work?" said Dr Matous.
paragraphs.zh: "团队不断组建、合并和重组。员工被重新安置，办公空间被重新设计。所有这些举措都是为了提升工作效率、协作和凝聚力。但这一切真的有效吗？"马图斯博士说道。

paragraphs.en: The study found that team-building exercises which focused on the sharing of and intervening into personal attitudes and relationships between team members were considered too heavy-handed and intrusive, although the researchers say some degree of openness and vulnerability is often necessary to make deep, effective connections with colleagues. "Some participants were against team-building exercises because they were implicitly compulsory. They didn't welcome management's interest in their lives beyond their direct work performance," said Matous. "Many people don't want to be forced into having fun or making friends, especially not on top of their busy jobs. They feel management is being too nosy or trying to control their lives too much."
paragraphs.zh: 研究发现，那些侧重于分享和干预团队成员个人态度及人际关系的团建活动，被认为过于强势和具有侵犯性，尽管研究人员表示，要与同事建立深入有效的联系，某种程度的坦率和示弱往往是必要的。马图斯说："一些参与者之所以反对团建活动，是因为它们实际上带有强制性。他们并不欢迎管理层越过其直接工作表现去关心他们的生活。许多人不想被迫去玩乐或交友，尤其是在繁忙的工作之余。他们觉得管理层太过好事，或试图过度控制他们的生活。"

paragraphs.en: In this study the researchers recommended a self-disclosure approach where participants were guided through a series of questions that allowed them to increasingly disclose personal information and values. The method is well-tested and has been shown to increase interpersonal closeness. However, to be successful it must be voluntary.
paragraphs.zh: 在这项研究中，研究人员推荐了一种自我表露的方法，即引导参与者回答一系列问题，使他们逐步分享更多个人信息和价值观。这种方法已经过充分检验，被证实可以增进人际亲密度。然而，要取得成功，这一过程必须是自愿的。

words.meaning: n. 自我表露；自我揭示

paragraphs.en: The researchers said there are numerous schools of thought that propose differing psychological methods for strengthening relationships. "With caution, many relational methods to improve teams and organisations can be borrowed from other fields. The question is how to apply them effectively to strengthen an entire collective, which is more than just the sum of individual relationships," said Dr Matous.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人员表示，有众多学术流派提出了各自不同的心理学方法来增进人际关系。马图斯博士说："若能谨慎行事，我们可以从其他领域借鉴许多用于改善团队和组织的关系方法。问题在于如何有效应用它们来巩固整个集体，因为集体并不仅仅是个体关系的总和。"
